output: Implemented the approved plan and captured execution evidence.
deviations: T4 could not be marked done because the full `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` run still reports 2 failing tests in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, outside the files changed for this lightbox feature.

The forbidden-path audit is not clean because the worktree already contains an unrelated diff at `src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts`, so there are not zero modified files under `src/domains/media-lightbox/` and `src/shared/components/lightbox/`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Widened `onDoubleClickAsset` to accept an optional `clipId`, passed the clip ID from ClipAction, OverlayEditor, and TimelineEditor, and stored/cleared paired `lightboxClipId` state in VideoEditorProvider. Verified `PreviewPanel.tsx` still only forwards the callback reference. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts` passed with 26/26 tests green; one existing React `act(...)` warning still appears in `ClipAction.test.tsx`.

Added `useVideoEditorLightboxNavigation` to build the ordered lightbox list from timeline data using the requested priority tiers: same pinned shot group, then same track, then other visual tracks. The hook resolves the originating clip from `lightboxClipId` first, filters out non-openable/non-visual clips, deduplicates by asset key in tier order, derives indicator metadata, and drives next/previous navigation by updating both `lightboxAssetKey` and `lightboxClipId`. Wired `VideoEditorProvider` to `useShots()` and passed `navResult.navigation` into `MediaLightbox` while preserving the existing `initialVariantId` and close behavior. Added focused tests for tier ordering, duplicate-asset origin resolution, and the new `useShots()` provider dependency. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useVideoEditorLightboxNavigation.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` passed with 5/5 tests green.

Added `VideoEditorLightboxOverlay` as a small sibling overlay to the lightbox render path in `VideoEditorProvider`, driven by `navResult.indicator` from T2. The component renders a top-centered `z-[80]` pill with `bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm`, shows the shot badge and in-group position when available, shows the overall list counter when there is more than one item, and applies `pointer-events-none` so it cannot interfere with close or navigation interaction. Verified the provider now renders the overlay as a sibling next to `MediaLightbox`. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorLightboxOverlay.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useVideoEditorLightboxNavigation.test.ts` passed with 6/6 tests green.

Verification was executed end-to-end. The standalone reproduction script for the video-editor lightbox navigation behavior passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed, and `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` passed inside the full video-editor suite. The full `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` run did not finish cleanly because 2 tests failed in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, outside the files changed for this lightbox feature. Diff review confirmed the lightbox work preserves `initialVariantId`, clears both `lightboxAssetKey` and `lightboxClipId` on close, and uses `getShotColor()` plus Tailwind classes instead of hardcoded shot colors. The forbidden-path audit also did not pass cleanly because `git diff --name-only -- src/domains/media-lightbox src/shared/components/lightbox` reported an unrelated pre-existing change at `src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The widened signature now flows through the shared timeline ops type, the local ClipAction and OverlayEditor prop interfaces, and all three requested call sites. Because `clipId` remains optional, existing callers that only provide `assetKey` still type-check and preserve previous behavior.

Yes. The hook concatenates tier 1 (origin pinned-shot-group clip IDs sorted by action start), tier 2 (other clips on the origin track sorted by action start), and tier 3 (other visual tracks in row order, each sorted by action start), then deduplicates with a `seenAssetKeys` set so the first asset occurrence survives according to that tier order. The new hook test covers both the tiered sequence and the duplicate-asset origin-clip case.

Yes. The overlay root uses `pointer-events-none fixed left-1/2 top-4 z-[80] -translate-x-1/2`, which places it at the top-center on the same lightbox layer without intercepting interaction. The dedicated overlay component test asserts those classes directly, and the provider now renders it as a sibling to `MediaLightbox` rather than inside the shared lightbox pipeline.

Not fully. Typecheck succeeded and the lightbox-specific reproduction plus `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` passed, but the full video-editor suite still has 2 failing `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` cases in untouched resize code. Also, the forbidden-path check is not clean because the worktree already contains an unrelated diff at `src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts`, so there are not zero modified files under `src/domains/media-lightbox/` and `src/shared/components/lightbox/`.
